items.title: NEW YORK'S HISTORIC RUN CONTINUES 🗽
items.author: NBA
items.body_excerpt:
NEW YORK'S HISTORIC RUN CONTINUES 🗽

With back-to-back road wins to open the NBA Finals, the Knicks become just the SECOND team in NBA history to win 13 consecutive games in a postseason, joining the 2017 Warriors (15)!

NYK (2-0) returns home for Game 3 on Monday at 8:30pm/et on ABC and ESPN 🏆

items.body_excerpt:
Wemby had 52 touches in G2 that never touched the paint - his most in a playoff game this season. 

OG scrams Brunson out of the transition cross-match. Wemby launces a three. Spurs don't hard crash, even though Wemby is ATB to get back as a backstop.

items.title: June 6, 1976: The NBA Champion Boston Celtics are the last team awarded the ORIGINAL Walter A. Brown Trophy.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
June 6, 1976: The NBA Champion Boston Celtics are the last team awarded the ORIGINAL Walter A. Brown Trophy.

That trophy was passed from the previous season’s champs to new champions each year (like the Stanley Cup).

In 1977, it was redesigned and reproduced each year for the NBA champions.

items.title: June 6, 1989: Finals MVP Joe Dumars of the Pistons beats the Lakers' James Worthy and scores during Game 1 of the NBA Finals.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
June 6, 1989: Finals MVP Joe Dumars of the Pistons beats the Lakers' James Worthy and scores during Game 1 of the NBA Finals.

Dumars finished with 22 PTS (11-16 FG)/7 AST and Isiah Thomas had 24 PTS/9 AST in Detroit's 109-97 win. Magic Johnson had 17 PTS/14 AST for Los Angeles.

items.body_excerpt:

Portland Mayor Neil Goldschmidt proclaimed the day "Blazer Victory Day" in the city.
